events.title: Iran Launches Barrage of Missiles and Drones at Bahrain
events.desc: Iranian state media reported that Tehran launched a barrage of missiles and drones targeting a US naval support base in Bahrain's capital. Sirens were heard in the capital following the reported attack.

events.title: US Military Launches More Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: The U.S. military announced it was conducting further strikes against Iran, which began Tuesday afternoon Eastern Time. These strikes come as the US prepares to reinstate a blockade of Iranian shipping.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Fire Amid Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US military conducted strikes, which Iran claimed were on US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman. This exchange of fire escalates tensions around the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Strikes 90 Iranian Targets Amidst Retaliation and Burial Ceremonies
events.desc: US Central Command reported hitting 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes. This action occurs concurrently with public mourning ceremonies for Iran's late supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, and follows earlier attacks.

events.title: Iran and US Exchange Strikes Following Tanker Incidents
events.desc: The US military stated it targeted missile sites and command centers in Iran, while Iranian sources claimed attacks on US bases. This follows incidents involving tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Continues Strikes Against Iran; Trump Threatens More
events.desc: President Trump indicated the US would likely conduct further strikes against Iran, following recent engagements. He stated that any future actions would be brief.

events.title: US Senate Democrats Block Defence Bill Over Iran War, Israel Integration
events.desc: The US Senate voted to block debate on the annual defence bill due to disagreements over war funding and provisions for defense cooperation with Israel, highlighting political divisions.

events.title: Israeli Strike Kills Seven in North Gaza Police Post
events.desc: An Israeli strike on a police post in northern Gaza resulted in the deaths of seven individuals, including a senior officer in the Hamas-run police force. The Israeli military stated the target was 'terrorists'.

events.title: US Lawmakers Propose Bill to Counter Transnational Repression by China and Iran
events.desc: A bipartisan bill was introduced in the US Congress to enhance penalties for foreign government agents involved in threatening individuals on US soil. This measure targets transnational repression tactics employed by countries like China and Iran.

events.title: Iraq PM States US Troops to Withdraw by September 30
events.desc: During a meeting with President Trump, the Iraqi Prime Minister announced that remaining American counter-ISIS troops would be withdrawn from Iraq by September 30. This announcement aligns with White House support for a withdrawal deadline.

events.title: US Senate Bill Blocks Debate on Defence Amidst Iran War Concerns
events.desc: US Senate Democrats blocked the annual defence bill, citing concerns over funding for the war with Iran and the integration of defense cooperation with Israel. This action highlights ongoing legislative debates regarding US foreign policy and military engagements.

events.title: UAE Condemns Iran's 'Brazen' Tanker Attack Amidst Fresh US Strikes
events.desc: The United Arab Emirates has condemned Iran's attack on tankers as 'brazen'. This condemnation comes as the US conducted its third consecutive night of strikes against Iran, following President Trump's announcement of a new blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Yemen's Houthis Launch Missiles at Saudi Arabia Post-Sanaa Airport Strikes
events.desc: Following reported strikes on Sanaa airport, the Iran-backed Houthi movement launched missiles targeting Abha airport in Saudi Arabia. The Houthis attribute the Saudi attack to Saudi Arabia and retaliated against the airport.

events.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps
events.desc: The UK has enacted a ban on providing support to Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). This move, utilizing new government powers, targets the group due to its alleged links to death threats and intimidation within Britain.

events.title: US Insists Strait of Hormuz Open Amidst Strikes with Iran
events.desc: US officials maintain that the Strait of Hormuz remains open, despite Iran's earlier claims of closing the strait and subsequent attacks on US allies and regional bases. This highlights conflicting narratives and ongoing military posturing.
